Name: WUQuickHealth
Description: 30-second triage snapshot: services, disk space, pending reboots, recent failures with HRESULT translation, cache size, and last update date. Routes tech to the correct follow-up scriptlet.

Param1.Name: DaysBack
Param1.Description: How many days of WU history to scan (default: 30)

Notes: v2.0: Complete rewrite replacing DiagnoseWindowsUpdate. Checks 4 core services (incl. UsoSvc), disk space (5/20 GB thresholds), 3 reboot signals (incl. Session Manager), WU history with 20-entry HRESULT translation table, SoftwareDistribution cache size, and last-success date. Read-only diagnostic.

Name: WUPolicyAudit
Description: Read-only audit of GPO, MDM/Intune, and UX-level policy settings governing Windows Update: WSUS routing, auto-update behavior, deferral periods, pause state, active hours, Delivery Optimization, target group, and split-brain GPO/MDM conflict detection with MDMWinsOverGP resolution.
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. Audits 3 registry layers: GPO (HKLM Policies WindowsUpdate), MDM (PolicyManager current device Update), and UX (WindowsUpdate UX Settings). Includes WSUS TCP reachability (3s timeout), ExcludeWUDriversInQualityUpdate from both GPO and MDM, and MDMWinsOverGP split-brain detection.

Name: WUNetworkCheck
Description: Tests whether the machine can reach Microsoft Update infrastructure. Checks DNS resolution, HTTPS connectivity (TCP 443), WinHTTP proxy, system proxy registry, PAC/WPAD auto-config, VPN adapter detection, and metered connection status.
Notes: Read-only network diagnostic. Tests 4 Microsoft Update endpoints + WSUS (if configured). Uses TcpClient with 5s timeout (not Invoke-WebRequest). Detects WinHTTP proxy via netsh, system proxy via HKLM registry, PAC/WPAD via AutoConfigURL and DefaultConnectionSettings blob. VPN detection via Get-NetAdapter keyword matching. Metered detection via DefaultMediaCost registry + Get-NetConnectionProfile. Does not check TLS/certs/clock (WU004 scope).

Name: DEFStatusTriage
Description: 30-second triage: Security Center AV products with bitmask decoding, Defender running mode, RTP, definitions, services, MDE sensor, and signal gap analysis. Routes tech to the correct follow-up scriptlet.
Notes: v2.0: Complete rewrite. Replaces DiagnoseDefenderSensor. Added Security Center bitmask decoding, AMRunningMode passive detection, ghost registration detection, MDE Sense check, signal gap analysis, NEXT footer routing. Read-only diagnostic.

Name: DEFDefinitionHealth
Description: Read-only diagnostic of the Defender definition update pipeline: signature age (hourly precision for AV, Antispyware, NIS), update source configuration (GPO FallbackOrder, ForceUpdateFromMU, polling interval), CDN connectivity (definitionupdates.microsoft.com, go.microsoft.com), recent update failure events with HRESULT translation, and scheduled task health (existence, state, last run result).
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. Checks 3 signature types with hourly precision, GPO update source configuration including Co-Management sabotage detection, TCP 443 to Microsoft CDN endpoints (3s timeout), last 48h of Defender operational event log for Event 2001/2003 failures, and 4 Windows Defender scheduled tasks. Does not duplicate WSUS reachability (see WU002).

Name: DEFThirdPartyAV
Description: Detects third-party AV conflicts, ghost Security Center registrations, uninstalled AV remnants (registry, services, filter drivers), and legacy DisableAntiSpyware/DisableAntiVirus policy overrides.
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. 4 check groups: (1) Security Center deep enumeration with bitmask decode, exe path validation, instance GUID reporting, (2) AV remnant scan across 10 vendors (registry paths, leftover services, leftover filter drivers in System32\drivers), (3) Ghost registration analysis cross-referencing Security Center with Defender AMRunningMode, (4) Defender policy overrides (DisableAntiSpyware, DisableAntiVirus) at GPO and MDM paths with entanglement detection. Does not perform remediation (DEF008 scope).

Name: WingetUpgradeSystemSilent
Description: Runs winget upgrade --all as SYSTEM to silently update all machine-wide installed applications (Program Files). No UAC prompt. Skips user-scoped apps — use WingetUpgradeUserApps for those.
Notes: v1.2: Runs as SYSTEM to avoid UAC for machine-wide apps. Uses -AllUsers on Get-AppxPackage. Added --disable-interactivity.

Name: WingetUpgradeUserApps
Description: Runs winget upgrade --all --scope user as the logged-on user to update user-scoped applications installed in AppData (Spotify, Discord, VS Code user installs, etc.). Complements WingetUpgradeAllSilent which handles machine-wide apps.
Notes: Runs as logged-on user via Invoke-AsUser. Uses --scope user to only touch AppData installs. No UAC prompt needed.

Name: IntuneForceComplianceCheck
Description: Triggers a forced Intune compliance evaluation by invoking the Intune Management Extension agent with the synccompliance URI.
Script:
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'

$imePath = 'C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Intune Management Extension\Microsoft.Management.Services.IntuneWindowsAgent.exe'

if (-not (Test-Path -Path $imePath)) {
    Write-Output ''
    Write-Output '[ERR] Intune Management Extension not found.'
    Write-Output "      Expected at: $imePath"
    Write-Output '      This device may not be Intune-enrolled.'
    Write-Output ''
    return
}

Write-Output ''
Write-Output '=== Intune Compliance Sync ==='
Write-Output ''

try {
    Start-Process -FilePath $imePath -ArgumentList 'intunemanagementextension://synccompliance'
    Write-Output '[OK] Compliance sync triggered successfully.'
    Write-Output '     The device will evaluate compliance policies shortly.'
}
catch {
    Write-Output "[ERR] Failed to trigger compliance sync: $($_.Exception.Message)"
}

Write-Output ''
Notes: Runs as SYSTEM. The IME agent handles the compliance evaluation internally.

Name: BLStatusSnapshot
Description: Triage snapshot: volume encryption status with ghost-state detection, OS drive letter validation, last BitLocker event, and BDESVC service health. Routes tech to the correct follow-up scriptlet.
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. Primary API is Get-BitLockerVolume with WMI Win32_EncryptableVolume fallback. Ghost state detection (encrypted + protection off) is a critical finding.

Name: BLTpmHealth
Description: Read-only diagnostic of the Trusted Platform Module (TPM): presence and state (TpmPresent, TpmReady, TpmEnabled, TpmActivated, TpmOwned with auto-provisioning check), specification version (1.2 vs 2.0 via Win32_Tpm WMI), manufacturer firmware vulnerability scanning (Infineon ROCA CVE-2017-15361, STMicro TPM-FAIL CVE-2019-16863), lockout state with estimated heal time calculation, and attestation/provisioning readiness (TBS service, owner auth retention level).
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. Uses Get-Tpm cmdlet with tpmtool.exe fallback when PowerShell module is unavailable. Checks 5 areas: TPM presence/state, TPM spec version (via Win32_Tpm WMI SpecVersion), firmware vulnerability scanning against known CVEs, lockout state with heal time calculation, and provisioning readiness (TBS service, auto-provisioning, owner auth level). Early exit if TPM not present. Cannot fix BIOS-level TPM issues -- script clearly says when the tech needs to enter BIOS setup.

Name: BLHardwarePrereqs
Description: Validates hardware and firmware prerequisites for BitLocker: UEFI vs Legacy BIOS, Secure Boot, GPT vs MBR, system partition, Modern Standby, and OEM-specific quirk detection.
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. 6 check groups: (1) boot mode UEFI/Legacy via Confirm-SecureBootUEFI with bcdedit fallback, (2) Secure Boot status with registry cross-reference, (3) disk partition scheme GPT/MBR via Get-Disk with CIM fallback, (4) system partition validation (EFI partition, size, FAT32 format), (5) Modern Standby via powercfg /a + CsEnabled registry + PlatformAoAcOverride override, (6) OEM identification with Dell/Lenovo/HP quirk advisories. Clearly distinguishes BIOS-level fixes from PowerShell-fixable issues.

Name: FWStatusTriage
Description: Triage snapshot: firewall profile status with active adapter correlation, Security Center cross-reference for ghost detection and Intune desync, MpsSvc service health, and network adapter inventory. Routes tech to the correct follow-up scriptlet.
Notes: Read-only diagnostic. Cross-references Get-NetFirewallProfile with Get-NetConnectionProfile to determine which profiles are actively filtering. Compares against Security Center WMI to detect Intune compliance desync.

Name: FWPolicyConflict
Description: Read-only diagnostic that reads firewall settings from all three policy sources (Local registry, GPO registry, MDM registry) and displays them side-by-side for each profile (Domain, Private, Public). Detects EnableFirewall=0 from any source, checks MDMWinsOverGP conflict resolution status with confirmation key validation, and identifies orphaned GPO tattooing on non-domain-joined machines.
Notes: Registry-read-only diagnostic. Reads from 3 sources: Local (CurrentControlSet\SharedAccess\Parameters\FirewallPolicy), GPO (SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\WindowsFirewall), MDM (SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\current\device\Firewall). Checks MDMWinsOverGP with ProviderSet/WinningProvider confirmation. Detects orphaned GPO keys on non-domain-joined machines. Does not query live firewall state (that is FW001's job). Does not remediate (that is FW006's job).
